title: fix(sec): upgrade gopkg.in/yaml.v3 to 3.0.0
body:
### What happened？
There are 1 security vulnerabilities found in gopkg.in/yaml.v3 v3.0.0-20200313102051-9f266ea9e77c
- [CVE-2022-28948](https://www.oscs1024.com/hd/CVE-2022-28948)


### What did I do？
Upgrade gopkg.in/yaml.v3 from v3.0.0-20200313102051-9f266ea9e77c to 3.0.0 for vulnerability fix

### What did you expect to happen？
Ideally, no insecure libs should be used.

### The specification of the pull request
[PR Specification](https://www.oscs1024.com/docs/pr-specification/) from OSCS

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *chncaption* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign umohnani8 for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This should (eventually? now?) be fixed by just updating the linter, instead.

Actually, we are _already_ using a more recent version: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/f694eca9cd885bb38518b121534cf11c8e11be4a/tests/tools/vendor/modules.txt#L601 .

So this PR seems to be an outcome of a misunderstanding of the details of how vendoring, and/or how to detect which version is used — or, quite likely, the consequence of an incorrectly implemented vulnerability scanning tool.
